ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Mɩrɩsɩ hʋ Moosi gyɩ aa bine dɩ nala kɩ tɩŋa abee Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala wɩbasɩya hʋ buloŋ, ba wɩnyuu rɛ yaa wɩɩ no: Ɩ aa bee kyɛ wɩɩ ta kpaa ʋ yaa ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna. Ma tɩŋ dimbeenyʋwa hʋ aa bɩ gyalɩya a gyʋʋ wɩɩsɩbee Luka
translated: And Moses' father-in-law said to him, "What you are doing is not good, for you and your people will wear yourselves out, for the thing is too heavy for you, you cannot do it alone, now listen to me, I will give you counsel and God will be with you."

ref: After Jesus sat down and told the twelve disciples to gather around him, he said, “If you want the place of honor, you must become a slave and serve others!”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu hɔŋ aŋ yɩrɩ ʋ hatɩnna fi abee balɩya hʋ, a basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Nal hʋ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ yaa ma tɩyaŋ nɩbal, see dɩ ʋ kaŋ ʋ tɩɩ bɩrɩmɩ ma tɩyaŋ haŋbiye a tʋma tɩya ma buloŋ.”
translated: And Jesus said to him, "Let me go and heal your daughter," but the ruler said, "My daughter has just died, but come and lay your hand on her and she will live."

ref: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
text: aŋ kpa ŋ we nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma nosi tɩyaŋ. Ba sɩ yaa ŋ sɩya, a vɩɩrɩ ŋ, aŋ kpaasɩ ŋ mal daagarɩɩ tɩyaŋ. Amɛ ʋ kyɛtoo kyɛɛ Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kyisi ŋ sʋʋ tɩyaŋ.” Zebedi haaŋ sʋla Yesu kɩ kyɛ lɩhɔnɩya dɩ ʋ tɩya ʋ balɩya Maakɩ
translated: And he went to his home town and his disciples followed him. On the Sabbath he began to teach in the synagogue and many who heard him were astonished, saying, “Where did this man get these things? What is this wisdom that has been given to him? What deeds of power are being done by his hands!"

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Wɩya no buloŋ kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ haluwokannɩ luwol aa piile kɩ wɩɩ.
translated: The child is not a fool, he knows how to take care of himself.

ref: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ, Ɛlaagya rɛ kɔnɩ sɩ laa sɩya ko, a kaŋ kɩŋ buloŋ marɩ biŋ.
translated: And Jesus said, “Truly I tell you, if you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Nyusʋŋtɩmma rɛ yaa nala hʋ aa wii, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ fɩyɛlɩ ba bembiye.
translated: The news that came yesterday, says that the Whites have started the war.

ref: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
text: Ba kasɩ ba a ŋmaa kɩdɩgɩ, a kpʋ kɩdɩgɩ, aŋ yaga kɩdɩgɩ hʋ mɛ abee bʋwa.
translated: They do not know how to farm, they do not know how to gather, but they know how to beg for food.

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Farasiima sii lɩɩ a vʋʋlɩ gɛɛ hʋ ba aa sɩ magɩsɩ Yesu a bara kana ʋ abee wɩpɩyɛsɩya.
translated: And it came to pass, as they departed from him, the angels said to the shepherds, Let us go to Bethlehem and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Dɩ bee nala rɛ we doŋ aŋ bɩ kaŋ nyʋʋdɩgɩ, ba bee hʋ sɩ sɩŋ?
translated: What is it that we have done to you, that you treat us like this?

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Aŋka baal hʋ vɩya aŋ kaŋ wɩɩ hʋ lɩɩ basɩ a taalɩ lee buloŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ Yesu bɩl bɩ wuwo dɩ ʋ gyʋʋ tɔɔ buloŋ tɩyaŋ. Amɛ ʋ aa hɔŋ beye samanɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ, ka nala lɩɩ lee kɛ buloŋ a kɩ ko ʋ lee.
translated: And behold, a woman who had a flow of blood for twelve years came behind Him and touched the hem of His garment. For she said to herself, "If only I may touch His garment, I shall be made well." But Jesus turned around, and when He saw her He said, "Be of good cheer, daughter; your faith has made you well." And the woman was made well from that hour.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: Amɛ sakuuribiye maga dɩ ʋ mɛ kɩɩ dɩdagɩl hʋ aa daga ʋ rɛ. Yoŋ mɛ maga dɩ ʋ kɩɩ nal hʋ aa deŋe ʋ rɛ. Dɩ ba aa rɛ yaŋ kaŋ dɩya tɩɩna kɩ yɩrɩ Sɩtaanɩ, ɛɛ kɛ, ba sɩ tɩya dɩya hʋ nala fene aa lɔrɔ te Sɩtaanɩ. Nal hʋ aa maga dɩ ma kɩ fá ʋ Luka
translated: And the angel said to them, "Fear not, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy that will be for all the people. For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Savior, who is Christ the Lord. And this will be a sign for you: you will find a baby wrapped in swaddling cloths and lying in a manger." And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God and saying, "Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased." And the shepherds went with haste to see this thing that had happened, which the Lord had made known to them, as it is written in the book of the prophet Luke

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: Ba aa na ʋ dɩ ʋ aa vala nɩɩ hʋ nyuu, ba bɩɩna anɩɩ nɩdɩma rɛ. Kambɩŋ kaŋ ba, ba yaga kyiyesuu.
translated: When they came to the place where the river flows, they saw many fish in the river. The hunters then shot them.

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Aŋka Yesu ha vɩya aŋ kɩ deŋ dɩ ʋ na nal hʋ a dige ʋ.
translated: And Jesus said to them, let us go to the other side.

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: Baala hʋ aa laa hal mʋ baga hʋ, ʋ tuŋ ba kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ kyɛdɩgɩ tuno.
translated: The child is crying because his mother has gone to the farm, and he is being taken care of by an elderly woman.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Badɔmɔŋ mɛ tele bʋnyɩŋ tɩyaŋ. Bʋnyɩŋ hʋ fa bɩ kaŋ taŋha yʋga. Ɛɛ wɩya, kɩna hʋ teŋ pesi nyʋ. Amɛ ba naaliliye bɩ wuwo gyʋʋ taŋha.
translated: The child is crying loudly outside. The child is crying because he wants his mother's breast. When he cries, people will come and carry him. They will take him to where his mother is breastfeeding.

ref: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ sii mʋ Pilato lee a sʋla ʋ dɩ ʋ leŋ dɩ ʋ kpa Yesu mʋ hogo. Ɛɛ rɛ Pilato laa nyʋwa anɩɩ ba mʋ kpa tɩya ʋ.
translated: And they were trying to accuse Jesus before Pilato but they did not find any guilt in him. And Pilato told them to take him away and judge him according to their law.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ŋ sɩ ko leŋ dɩ ʋ deye.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, “Follow me and I will make you fishers of men.”

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Dɩ Wɩɩsɩ rɛ fa bɩ kere kyɛyɛ hʋ wɩya no aa sɩ yaa ta, nɩdɩgɩ mɛ fa bɩ sɩ ka beewɩya tʋwara hʋ aa sɩ ko sɩ lɔrɩ rɛ kɩŋkaŋ. Amɛ akuu nala hʋ ʋ aa lɩya wɩya, ʋ sɩ kere kyɛyɛ hʋ ta.
translated: When the rain falls, it does not fall on one man's head alone, it also falls on the plants and other things, and that is how God shows his love to all of us, he does not love one person and hate another.

ref: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ kɩdɩgɩ lɩɩ ʋ takoobii a ŋmaŋ baal kɩdɩgɩ dɩgɩŋ kyɔl. Baal no fa yaa Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ dɩya tʋŋtʋnnɩ rɛ.
translated: And when Jesus had finished speaking these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching, for he taught them as one having authority.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Gyɔɔŋ hatɩnna hʋ aa nɩɩ wɩɩ no, ba sii ko a laa ʋ teŋbii a kaŋ mʋ hogo. Yesu tɩya nala tusi bʋnɔŋ kɩdiiliye ba di vɔgɔ Matiwu
translated: When Jesus had finished speaking, he went with his disciples to the other side of the Jordan River to the region of Judea
